source_pr.title: session: fix a bug when updating duplicate bindings
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #25161 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

When dealing with duplicate binding, the situation of different statuses is not considered. Previously, the first binding was retained. 
Now only bindings whose status is `using` and `builtin` are retained.

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:

How it Works:

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- Integration test


Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- session: fix a bug when updating duplicate bindings

symptoms: r := mustExecSQL(c, se, `select original_sql, bind_sql, default_db, status, create_time from mysql.bind_info where source != 'builtin' order by create_time`) (session/bootstrap_test.go)
failure_signature: r := mustExecSQL(c, se, `select original_sql, bind_sql, default_db, status, create_time from mysql.bind_info where source != 'builtin' order by create_time`) (session/bootstrap_test.go)
fix_pattern: r := mustExecSQL(c, se, `select original_sql, bind_sql, default_db, status, create_time from mysql.bind_info where source != 'builtin' order by create_time`) (session/bootstrap_test.go)

evidence: evidence: r := mustExecSQL(c, se, `select original_sql, bind_sql, default_db, status, create_time from mysql.bind_info where source != 'builtin' order by create_time`) (session/bootstrap_test.go)
